transcript: 藢蓻spri藧 d蓹 藞k蓴藧r
definitions: A feeling of pride, fellowship, and common loyalty shared by the members of a particular group.
The common spirit existing in the members of a group and inspiring enthusiasm, devotion, and strong regard for the honor of the group.
A sense of group identity and solidarity.
examples: The team's esprit de corps was evident in their synchronized movements.
Building esprit de corps among the new recruits was the sergeant's main goal.
Lack of esprit de corps led to internal conflicts.
